**Exploring color mirroring**

I’m considering a rule for mirroring a unique color pixel across a defined axis, like vertical or horizontal, based on colored border stripes. Rule: Identify any full-length stripe(s) of a single colour that touch the border.  Each such stripe is an axis of reflection.  Mirror every interior coloured cell (those not belonging to the border stripes) across the nearest stripe:  • If the stripe is vertical, copy the cell to the horizontally opposite side of that stripe, preserving its row.  • If the stripe is horizontal, copy the cell to the vertically opposite side of that stripe, preserving its column.  • Cells that land on another stripe or outside the grid are discarded.  A cell is never moved twice and stripes themselves remain unchanged.
rule_evaluation_reasoning: The Evaluated Rule describes a mirroring transformation based on border stripes, which is fundamentally different from the Ground Truth Rule that focuses on moving pixels to the center between parallel lines of the same color. The two rules operate under different principles and do not achieve the same transformation.
